title: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) Up 3.1% Since Last Earnings Report: Can It Continue?
text: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) reported earnings 30 days ago. What's next for the stock?

title: Here's Why Investors Should Retain Norwegian Cruise (NCLH) Now
text: Norwegian Cruise's (NCLH) emphasis on onboard revenue generation bodes well. However, volatility in inflation, rising fuel prices and rising interest rates are a concern.

title: Cruise Stocks Expect Tailwind Profits As Credit Card Travel Spending Runs Hot This Summer
text: Carnival Corp. CCL, Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. NCLH, and Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. RCL stand to benefit from an increase in spending in the coming months.

title: Bank News and a New Normal for Investors to Consider
text: Three of the four largest bank failures in U.S. history have happened in the past two months.

title: Cruise Lines See Smoother Seas On Horizon As Profitablity Returns
text: If you look out over the horizon, you may just spot the cruise line industry floating along on a life raft, after taking on water and gradually sinking in recent years.

title: Norwegian Cruise Line Offers Value
text: The cruise industry faced severe setbacks in 2020 with travel restrictions and health concerns surrounding the Covid-19 virus leading to the suspension of operations and the cancellation of numerous bookings. However, with the gradual reopening of economies and the restoration of consumer confidence, the industry is making a comeback and regaining some lost ground.

title: Why Norwegian Cruise Line Stock Jumped Monday
text: Norwegian Cruise Line exceeded first-quarter estimates and sees a better year than anticipated. CEO Frank Del Rio is leaving but will remain on as an advisor through 2025.

title: Norwegian Cruise (NCLH) Q1 Earnings Top Estimates, Rise Y/Y
text: Norwegian Cruise's (NCLH) first-quarter 2023 performance benefits from strong consumer demand, solid booking environment and robust onboard revenue generation.

title: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) Reports Q1 Loss, Tops Revenue Estimates
text: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) came out with a quarterly loss of $0.30 per share versus the Zacks Consensus Estimate of a loss of $0.43. This compares to loss of $1.82 per share a year ago.

title: Norwegian Cruise Line stock surges after losses narrow more than forecast and revenue more than triples
text: Shares of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. NCLH ran up 2.9% toward a 7-week high in premarket trading Monday, after the cruise operator reported first-quarter results that beat expectations and provided an upbeat full-year outlook.
